title: HL7 Message Delivery Monitoring
spl:
index=interfaces sourcetype="hl7:v2"
| eval ack_ok=if(ACK_Code="AA",1,0)
| timechart span=15m sum(ack_ok) as ok, count as total
| eval ack_rate=round(ok/total*100,2)
| where ack_rate < 99
description: ADT/ORM gaps break registration, billing, and care coordination workflows.
value: ADT/ORM gaps break registration, billing, and care coordination workflows.
implementation: Track message control ID retries; alert on NAK storms or queue depth from engine metrics.
visualization: Line chart (ACK rate), Table (top failing interfaces), Single value (queue depth).
knownFalsePositives: HL7 retries after interface engine upgrades, queue backlogs, and vendor test messages; use interface stats before assuming patient-safety impact.
detailedImplementation:
Prerequisites
• Install and configure the required add-on or app: Interface engine (Mirth, Cloverleaf).
• Ensure the following data sources are available: `sourcetype="hl7:v2"`.
• For app installation, inputs.conf, and Splunk directory layout, see the Implementation guide: docs/implementation-guide.md

Step 1 — Configure data collection
Track message control ID retries; alert on NAK storms or queue depth from engine metrics.

Step 2 — Create the search and alert
Run the following SPL in Search (then save as report or alert; adjust time range and threshold as needed):

```spl
index=interfaces sourcetype="hl7:v2"
| eval ack_ok=if(ACK_Code="AA",1,0)
| timechart span=15m sum(ack_ok) as ok, count as total
| eval ack_rate=round(ok/total*100,2)
| where ack_rate < 99
```

Understanding this SPL

**HL7 Message Delivery Monitoring** — ADT/ORM gaps break registration, billing, and care coordination workflows.

Documented **Data sources**: `sourcetype="hl7:v2"`. **App/TA** (typical add-on context): Interface engine (Mirth, Cloverleaf). The SPL below should target the same indexes and sourcetypes you configured for that feed—rename `index=` / `sourcetype=` if your deployment differs.

The first pipeline stage scopes events using **index**: interfaces; **sourcetype**: hl7:v2. That sourcetype matches what this use case lists under Data sources.

**Pipeline walkthrough**

• Scopes the data: index=interfaces, sourcetype="hl7:v2". Cross-check against **Data sources** above so indexes and sourcetypes match your ingestion.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ack_ok** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• `timechart` plots the metric over time using **span=15m** buckets — ideal for trending and alerting on this use case.
• `eval` defines or adjusts **ack_rate** — often to normalize units, derive a ratio, or prepare for thresholds.
• Filters the current rows with `where ack_rate < 99` — typically the threshold or rule expression for this monitoring goal.


Step 3 — Validate
Run the search on recent production data, spot-check a handful of results against the source system or operational runbooks (cash operations, EHR, payment gateway, etc.), and adjust thresholds to match your seasonal patterns and test windows so teams are not flooded with noise.

Step 4 — Operationalize
Add the search to a dashboard or set up alert actions (email, webhook, PagerDuty, etc.) as required. Document the use case in your runbook and assign an owner. Consider visualizations: Line chart (ACK rate), Table (top failing interfaces), Single value (queue depth).
grandmaExplanation: In Healthcare, we pay close attention to "HL7 Message Delivery Monitoring" in our day-to-day work so that fraud, waste, and compliance issues do not slip by quietly. We help teams focus on the few situations that need a person, not every routine blip in the data.
dataModelAcceleration: Not applicable for the primary search: it relies on application- and industry-specific log formats, not a normalized CIM data model. Optional mapping to CIM, if you adopt it, is a separate follow-on project.
